explanation:
what i'm trying to wrap my head around, regards how, it seems, all of the pieces i've evaluated thus far have, basically, come from the same person, *maybe* the same family or "methodology"

as i said before: i acknowledge the craftsmanship; and i forecast, that in this curation (that's the word i was trying to think of, before), i won't deem a single one of these as [æsthetically-displeasing]

but, that still doesn't mean, i won't come up with the opinion, that none of them stand out

Iterations

even if the subject matter, the presentation, and the colouration, are different — so long as the core building blocks remain the same, then..
..all i can do, is conclude, i am doing nothing but looking at isomers

13 ounces of purple water is 13 ounces of purple water

if you freeze it as a cube, or a sphere, or as a star (bolide)



regarding this piece: the only thing really "unique" about it is it's not following the same format as the previous ones

obviously

not because i am a "minimalist" per se; but, i do appreciate how striking the two-toned drawings of the women looks

the calligraphy to the left, also, looks very nice — it takes skill (or a brain that's wired for it) to keep straight lines like that, unassisted
key_association_original: striking the two-toned drawings of the women looks
evidence_span: striking the two-toned drawings of the women looks
